commands.author: jimmypuckett

target.title: fix(msteams): handle message card submit values
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Microsoft Teams can deliver Adaptive Card submit clicks as normal `message` activities with empty `text` and submitted data in `activity.value`. The existing Teams handler already serialized the canonical `adaptiveCard/action` invoke path, but this observed fallback shape could reach the agent as an empty or no-op message. This PR reuses the existing Adaptive Card submit serialization for that normal-message fallback path, while preserving existing precedence so real typed message text and extracted HTML text still win over `activity.value`. ## Summary - Problem: Microsoft Teams Adaptive Card submit clicks can arrive as a normal `message` activity with empty text and the submit payload in `activity.value`, not only as the canonical Adaptive Card invoke shape. - What changed: shared Teams Adaptive Card submit serialization between the canonical invoke handler and the normal message handler fallback. - Why it matters: Teams button clicks that would otherwise look like empty inbound messages now reach the agent as meaningful user input. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes: N/A - Related: live Teams payload capture - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History - Root cause: the Teams monitor handler only converted Adaptive Card submit payloads to text on the canonical `adaptiveCard/action` invoke path. Live Teams testing showed that submit data can also arrive on a normal `message` activity as `activity.value`, with no useful `activity.text`. - Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered the canonical invoke path, but not a normal `message` activity whose useful content is only in `activity.value`. - Prior context: Teams button submit handling already existed for invoke activities; this patch reuses the same serializer for the observed fallback shape. - Why this surfaced now: live payload capture of Teams Adaptive Card actions found a delivery shape that the existing code did not explicitly handle. ## Regression Test Plan - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler.adaptive-card.test.ts` - Scenario the test locks in: - A Teams `message` activity with empty `text` and submit data in `activity.value` is delivered to the agent as serialized submit text. - Existing real message text still wins over `activity.value`, so normal messages are not overwritten by incidental structured values. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is inside Teams inbound message normalization; a focused monitor-handler regression catches the observed payload shape without widening the PR into broader Teams interactivity work. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Teams Adaptive Card button clicks delivered through the normal message fallback path now produce agent-visible input. - Existing canonical Adaptive Card invoke behavior is preserved. - Plain Teams message text continues to take precedence over `activity.value`. ## Evidence - [x] Failing/passing regression behavior covered by test - [x] Screenshot from live Microsoft Teams tenant - [x] Anonymized raw activity excerpt Live Teams proof, sanitized: - A Teams Adaptive Card rendered with a `Send fallback value` button. - After pressing the button, Teams showed `Your response was sent to the app`. - The test bot received the fallback payload and replied with: - `` - button label `"Send fallback value"` Anonymized raw activity excerpt from the payload capture: ```json { "type": "message", "text": "", "hasReplyToId": true, "value": { "value": "", "label": "Send fallback value" }, "channelId": "msteams", "conversationType": "personal" } ``` Screenshot evidence: - Sanitized screenshot attached to the PR comment/body after upload. ## Validation Passed: ```bash pnpm test -- extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler.adaptive-card.test.ts ``` Result: ```text Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 8 passed (8) ``` Live smoke: - Deployed this branch's `@openclaw/msteams` extension to a private test gateway for a single-account Teams test. - Sent a clean Adaptive Card to Teams. - Clicked `Send fallback value`. - Verified the test bot received and replied with the submitted fallback payload. - Restored the test gateway back to its prior multi-account Teams setup after screenshot capture. Rollback verification after smoke: - `Microsoft Teams default: enabled, configured, running, works` - `Microsoft Teams secondary: enabled, configured, running, works` - Both local Teams listener ports were active - Public unsigned probes for both Teams routes returned `401 Unauthorized`, not `502` Known validation limitation: ```bash pnpm --filter @openclaw/msteams exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit ``` currently fails on latest `upstream/main` in pre-existing Teams SDK drift areas unrelated to this change, including: - `doctor-contract-api.ts` - `src/graph-thread.ts` - `src/presentation.ts` - `src/probe.ts` - `src/thread-parent-context.ts` - `src/token.ts` The new/changed files for this PR are not among the reported typecheck failures. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No This is intentionally independent of the larger Teams multi-account PR. If that PR lands first, this branch should be rebased and the same fallback behavior carried into the refactored account-aware Teams monitor handler. The expected overlap is limited to the Teams monitor-handler files; the behavior remains the same. ## Implementation Alignment - Reuses the existing Teams Adaptive Card submit serialization behavior instead of introducing a second parser for the message fallback path. - Keeps the fallback inside the existing Teams message normalization flow, next to `activity.text`, HTML attachment text extraction, and mention stripping. - Uses existing OpenClaw runtime helpers (`isRecord`, `normalizeOptionalLowercaseString`, `normalizeOptionalString`) for unknown payload coercion. - Preserves existing precedence: typed message text and extracted HTML text win over `activity.value`. - Adds focused coverage in the existing Teams Adaptive Card monitor-handler test file. ## Risk and Mitigation - Risk: serializing `activity.value` too eagerly could turn unrelated structured message values into user text. - Mitigation: the fallback only runs when both extracted message text and HTML text are empty, and existing message text continues to win. - Risk: Teams has multiple Adaptive Card submit shapes. - Mitigation: the serializer is shared with the existing canonical invoke path and handles string, object, and Teams `msteams.imBack` submit payloads. ## AI Assistance This PR was prepared with AI assistance. The implementation, tests, live Teams validation, and anonymized evidence were reviewed by the contributor before submission.

commands.author: mjnkao

target.title: feat(durable): add opt-in agent coordination kernel
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw already has strong surfaces for sessions, background tasks, Task Flow, Workboard, subagents, and Gateway channels, but long-running multi-agent work can still become hard to reason about when it spans many steps or branches. The concrete failure mode this PR targets is coordinator silence: a parent turn starts child/subagent work, one child is still running or fails, the gateway restarts, and the operator no longer has a bounded durable projection of which run, child, step, or recovery state is open.

This PR adds an opt-in native durable coordination kernel for that missing layer. It is intentionally not a general-purpose Temporal/Restate clone and does not replace Task Flow/background tasks/Workboard. It gives those existing surfaces a shared local-first ledger for coordination identity, child links, fan-in readiness, restart recovery markers, and read-only observability.

Most useful validation evidence is below: focused regression tests cover the two ClawSweeper P1 state bugs, typecheck/build/guardrails pass, isolated enabled-runtime proof verifies Gateway projection and fan-in behavior, schema upgrade smoke preserves existing data, and a real OpenClaw A instance was updated to this branch and restarted with durable workflows enabled.

## Summary

This replaces the cumulative implementation stack from #97468, #97469, and #97476 with one self-contained vertical slice for agent review.

It adds an opt-in native durable agent coordination kernel that records:

- workflow runs, steps, events, refs, timers, signals, and child links;
- agent-turn lifecycle checkpoints;
- read-only Gateway coordination projection;
- CLI inspection helpers;
- subagent child-run recording and fan-in state.

The goal is not to replace Task Flow/background tasks/Workboard. This PR gives those surfaces a shared, bounded coordination ledger for restart-safe multi-agent work.

## Reviewer Feedback Addressed

- **No sidecar DB by default.** Durable tables live in the canonical shared state database at `state/openclaw.sqlite` via `resolveOpenClawStateSqlitePath(...)`.
- **Shared schema lifecycle.** Durable tables are added to `src/state/openclaw-state-schema.sql` and regenerated schema artifacts.
- **Disabled by default.** Runtime hooks and Gateway reads require `OPENCLAW_DURABLE_WORKFLOWS` to be explicitly enabled.
- **Disabled read is non-mutating.** `durable.coordination.get` returns disabled without opening/creating durable state; this has a regression test.
- **No raw subagent task persistence.** Subagent coordination metadata stores ids, bounded labels/names, and `taskHash`; it no longer stores the raw `task` body.
- **No ledger-only operator controls.** The initial CLI surface is read-only inspection; cancel/retry/resume/signal write controls were removed until they can route through the live task/agent runtime contract.
- **Active parent binding.** `sessions_spawn` now passes the requester run id into the durable subagent mirror, and the bridge falls back to newest same-session parent only when older call paths do not provide a run id.
- **Fan-in reopen.** Registering a later child now reconciles fan-in immediately and clears stale `completedAt` when a terminal fan-in step returns to `waiting_child`.

## User Impact

This targets the class of bugs where long-running or branching agent work becomes silent or hard to recover:

- parent agent waits on subagents but cannot tell which child is still open;
- one failed child should not block unrelated sibling completion;
- gateway restart should not erase where a coordinated run was;
- operators need a bounded timeline/projection instead of scraping sessions or plugin state.

## Scope Boundaries

- Feature is opt-in and experimental.
- Normal chat turns are unchanged while the flag is off.
- This does not add CLI write controls, retention/compaction, Postgres, or multi-worker leases.
- This does not change Task Flow or Workboard semantics yet; it provides primitives they can project onto later.

## Evidence

Local proof environment: macOS local worktree, Node v25.8.1, commit `4664e48f67e76f3f488b2f9e2fade3e54183c588`, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/var/folders/.../T` except for the OpenClaw A runtime smoke below.

| Reviewer ask | Command / proof | Observed result |
| --- | --- | --- |

commands.author: mjnkao

target.title: docs: propose durable agent coordination kernel
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw has several existing durable-ish surfaces: background tasks for detached work, Task Flow for multi-step orchestration, Workboard for operator/task visibility, sessions for transcript state, and subagents for delegation. The open architecture question is whether these surfaces should share a small native coordination kernel so branching agent work can expose parent/child identity, fan-in readiness, restart recovery markers, and bounded observability without turning OpenClaw into a general-purpose workflow platform.

This RFC is intentionally proposal-only. It does not claim the durable kernel already exists on `main`, and it does not ask to replace Task Flow, background tasks, Workboard, or sessions. It gives maintainers a focused architecture boundary to approve, redirect into Task Flow internals, or narrow into a shared task/session module before implementation PRs go deeper.

Most useful validation evidence for this docs PR is review-focused rather than runtime-focused: it replaces the earlier broad docs stack, keeps public docs language explicitly proposed, names non-goals, and asks maintainers to decide the storage/core boundary before any default-on behavior exists.

## Summary

This is a replacement RFC for the earlier durable-core docs PR (#97467). It keeps the proposal intentionally small and avoids describing implementation as if it already exists on `main`.

The RFC proposes a durable agent coordination kernel underneath existing OpenClaw surfaces:

- background tasks remain the detached-work ledger;
- Task Flow remains the multi-step orchestration surface;
- Workboard remains an optional operator/card surface;
- sessions remain the model-visible transcript boundary;
- the proposed kernel only owns coordination identity, event ordering, child links, fan-in readiness, restart recovery markers, and bounded observability.

## Why This Shape

ClawSweeper correctly noted that the previous docs stack was hard to review because it documented future code as present behavior and overlapped with Task Flow/product direction. This replacement is proposal-only and asks for maintainer alignment before deeper implementation.

## Non-Goals

- No Temporal/Restate/Hatchet/LangGraph clone.
- No default-on behavior change.
- No raw prompt/task persistence in coordination metadata.
- No plugin/dashboard/channel coupling.
- No claim that Task Flow or Workboard should be replaced.

## Review Focus

Please review whether this is the right architectural boundary for future work:

1. Should this live as a standalone core runtime module, inside Task Flow internals, or as a narrower shared task/session submodule?
2. Is shared `state/openclaw.sqlite` the right lifecycle home for the first local-first ledger?
3. Is the proposed primitive set small enough for upstream OpenClaw?

## Evidence

Docs-only RFC. No runtime behavior ships from this branch, so no runtime tests were run for this branch.

Validation evidence for this PR:

- The document is explicitly framed as an architecture proposal, not current product behavior.
- It preserves Task Flow, background tasks, Workboard, and sessions as existing supported surfaces.
- It narrows the durable-kernel ownership to coordination identity, event ordering, child links, fan-in readiness, restart recovery markers, and bounded observability.
- It lists non-goals to avoid implying a Temporal/Restate/Hatchet/LangGraph replacement or a default-on runtime change.
- The companion implementation PR #97508 carries runtime validation for the opt-in slice while this RFC remains the maintainer architecture decision point.

target.title: fix(mattermost): truncate draft previews on code-point boundaries
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Mattermost draft preview truncation used raw UTF-16 slicing. If an emoji or other astral character straddled the draft preview limit, the draft update could contain a dangling surrogate half before the ellipsis.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Mattermost draft text normalizer now uses the existing plugin SDK `sliceUtf16Safe` helper while preserving the existing max-character cap and trailing ellipsis behavior.

## User Impact

Long Mattermost draft updates containing emoji no longer produce malformed replacement characters at truncation boundaries.

## Evidence

- git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/draft-stream.test.ts
- .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main

Autoreview result: clean; no accepted/actionable findings.

## After-fix Proof

Boundary probe on this PR branch:

```json
{
  "usesSafeSlice": true,
  "output": "aaaaaaaa...",
  "outputLength": 11,
  "hasLoneSurrogate": false
}
```

The probe places an emoji across the `maxChars - 3` slice boundary. The after-fix output drops the emoji whole and leaves no lone UTF-16 surrogate.

commands.author: TurboTheTurtle

target.title: fix(agent): reset finalize retry transcript
target.body:
Closes #93166.

## What Problem This Solves
`before_agent_finalize` retries could leave the rejected first assistant answer as finalized session history before the revision pass, so the retry could see its own rejected draft as prior conversation and group-chat silence handling could collapse final delivery.

## Summary
When `before_agent_finalize` requests a revision, the runner now trims the rejected assistant leaf from the session transcript before dispatching the retry. The revision pass remains authoritative, including intentional `NO_REPLY`, and the rejected first answer is not restored.

## Evidence
A temporary embedded-runner proof against this branch showed the transcript rewrite at the retry boundary:

```text
proof after first attempt=["Question?","First answer."]
proof before revision attempt=["Question?"]
proof delivered payloads=[{"text":"Revised answer."}]
proof final transcript=["Question?","Revised answer."]
```

On rebased head `61d680f1bc`, hosted `Real behavior proof`, `check-test-types`, `Critical Quality (agent-runtime-boundary)`, and the full PR check rollup are passing.

target.title: fix(auth): allow OpenAI OAuth for audio transcription
target.body:
Closes #96135

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with an OpenAI/Codex OAuth profile configured for `tools.media.audio` would lose batch audio transcription when OpenClaw routed OpenAI audio through `openai-audio-transcriptions`.

The failing path rejected the selected OAuth profile before dispatch with: `requires an OpenAI API key profile`.

## Why This Change Was Made

`openai-audio-transcriptions` is now treated as an OpenAI bearer-auth API alongside `openai-chatgpt-responses`, so OAuth/token profiles can satisfy the shared model-auth guard for batch audio transcription. Direct OpenAI Platform model APIs such as `openai-responses` still require API-key auth.

The shared resolver now preserves shipped mixed-auth precedence for default/fallback profile resolution: if an OpenAI env API key such as `CODEX_API_KEY` or `OPENAI_API_KEY` is available, audio transcription keeps the env API key ahead of a default OAuth profile. Explicit locked OAuth profiles still use OAuth for `openai-audio-transcriptions`.

## User Impact

Users who intentionally use OpenAI/Codex OAuth for voice-note or batch audio transcription can use their selected OpenAI OAuth profile again instead of being blocked before transcription runs.

Existing mixed-auth users with an OpenAI OAuth default profile and an env API key keep API-key transcription behavior unless they explicitly lock the OAuth profile.

Users with OpenAI API keys are otherwise unaffected, and non-audio OpenAI Platform APIs remain API-key-only.

## Evidence

Focused tests:

```bash
env OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.local-no-auth.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.auto-audio.test.ts
# [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 48.90s
# media-understanding: 23 tests passed
# agents: 74 tests passed
```

Formatting and diff checks:

```bash
pnpm format:check -- src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.local-no-auth.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.auto-audio.test.ts
# All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
# clean
```

Autoreview:

```bash
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
# autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
# overall: patch is correct (0.82)
```

Dependency/source evidence checked:

- Official OpenAI OpenAPI spec from Stainless: global `ApiKeyAuth` is HTTP bearer auth, and `/audio/transcriptions` is the `createTranscription` endpoint.
- Installed official `openai` Node SDK generated source: `audio.transcriptions.create()` posts to `/audio/transcriptions` with `__security: { bearerAuth: true }`; `client.ts` sends bearer auth as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
- The linked issue includes direct runtime evidence that an OpenAI OAuth credential successfully returned HTTP 200 from `POST /v1/audio/transcriptions` with `model=gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, including a real uploaded voice-note case with API-key fallback disabled.

Live after-fix proof:

- A follow-up PR comment includes live OpenClaw CLI proof on `codex/fix-openai-oauth-transcription` at `7177136d859410c0f8b54c6d123bbab88b30ae2d` using `pnpm openclaw infer audio transcribe --model openai/gpt-4o-mini-transcribe --json`.
- The proof ran with `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_OAUTH_TOKEN`, `OPENAI_BASE_URL`, and `OPENAI_AUDIO_BASE_URL` unset, using isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>` and `OPENCLAW_AUTH_STORE_READONLY=1`. No OpenClaw auth profile was persisted after the run.
- The command returned `ok: true` with an `audio.transcription` output through the patched `openai-audio-transcriptions` path using OAuth-only credentials.
- ClawSweeper re-review on June 27, 2026 at 17:20 UTC marked contributor proof sufficient. The later mixed OAuth-plus-API-key precedence finding has now been addressed in commit `241a743566`.

target.title: improve: show edit mismatch candidate lines
target.body:
Closes #97032

AI-assisted by Codex.

## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where edit-tool exact-match failures only reported that `oldText` was not found and then dumped a capped file snippet. Users had no nearby line comparison to tell whether the miss was caused by indentation, escaping, whitespace, or similar text drift.

## Why This Change Was Made

The edit mismatch hint now adds up to three bounded closest candidate lines before the existing capped `Current file contents` section. Each candidate includes a line number, expected text, found text, a simple difference marker, and a short hint such as indentation mismatch or backslash escaping mismatch.

The same hint is now applied to indexed multi-edit `edits[n]` not-found failures when the index is in the real-edit apply path. Mixed no-op validation errors are guarded so they do not attach candidates from the wrong edit list.

The matching algorithm is unchanged. This is diagnostic-only output on the existing exact-match failure path.

## User Impact

Users and agents can recover from failed edits faster because the error points at likely nearby text and explains common mismatch classes instead of forcing blind retries.

## Evidence

- Pushed follow-up commit `77c059e7` after review feedback.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.ts src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /tmp/codex-fast-wrapper` reported clean after the indexed multi-edit and mixed no-op guard changes.
- Local terminal proof using production `createEditTool` against a temp file, with the temp path redacted:

```text
Could not find edits[1] in <tmp>/demo.txt. The oldText must match exactly including all whitespace and newlines.
Closest candidate lines for oldText:
- line 2:
  expected: "  const beta = 2;"
  found:    "const beta = 2;"
  diff:     ^^^^^^^^^^^^ ^^^^
  hint:     indentation differs (expected 2 leading whitespace chars, found 0)
```

target.title: fix: archive role-ordering reset transcripts
target.body:
Closes #97529

AI-assisted by Codex.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users whose sessions hit an automatic role-ordering-conflict reset would silently lose the previous session transcript. The reset replayed only a bounded message tail into the new session, then hard-deleted the full previous transcript instead of preserving it as a reset archive.

## Why This Change Was Made

The role-ordering reset lifecycle now uses the existing reset transcript archive path instead of the unlink-only cleanup path. This keeps the behavior aligned with adjacent reset and rollover lifecycle flows while preserving the bounded replay into the new session.

## User Impact

Users retain a recoverable `.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` archive for the full pre-reset transcript, so long conversations are not silently reduced to the replayed tail after this automatic recovery path runs.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts`
  - Passed: 1 test file, 58 tests.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts`
  - Passed with no output.
- `git diff --check`
  - Passed with no output.
- `git diff --numstat origin/main...HEAD`
  - `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts`: `11  1`
  - `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts`: `5  31`
- Branch-level real behavior proof on the fixed branch, driving the real exported `persistSessionResetLifecycle` from `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts` against an actual file-backed `agents/main/sessions/` layout:

```text
PR #97544 behavior proof: role-ordering reset archive path
workdir: /private/tmp/openclaw-97529-session-reset
tempRoot: /var/folders/ms/9qxgldzs4dd9f7hv74vrgxfh0000gn/T/openclaw-pr-97544-proof-TnZ4Gg
previous file exists after reset: false
reset archive files: 1 ["aaaaaaaa-1111-2222-3333-prevprevprev.jsonl.reset.2026-06-28T18-07-17.495Z"]
archive preserved previous session header: true
archive preserved user messages: 25
new transcript exists: true
replayed records returned: 6
new transcript replayed user messages: 3
new transcript includes newest user tail: true
```

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt 'Review the fix for GitHub issue #97529. Scope: preserve previous session transcript during role-ordering-conflict reset by archiving instead of unlinking, with focused session-accessor regression test. Focus on data-loss/session lifecycle regressions and whether proof covers the touched surface.'`
  - Clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported.

No live provider reproduction was run. The real behavior proof drives the fixed file-backed session lifecycle directly: it writes a 25-turn previous transcript in the real sessions directory shape, runs the reset lifecycle with cleanup enabled, verifies the previous transcript is preserved as exactly one `.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` archive, and verifies the replayed tail still lands in the new transcript.

target.title: feat(attach): node conduit + gateway-owned session hydration (pick-up-anywhere)
target.body:
> ⚠️ **Stacked on #96454** (PR2, the `openclaw attach` launcher) → **#96351** (PR1, the `attach` grant primitive). Merge after they land; the earlier commits here are theirs — review the `node.attach*`, hydration, `--via node` launcher, and admin-gate commits. Branch merges into current `main` with no conflicts.

## What Problem This Solves

A human-driven harness (Claude Code) on a machine that reaches the gateway **only through a node** — not a direct connection — can't use `openclaw attach`. And even where it could, there was no way to **continue the same conversation across surfaces**. This PR delivers both, over the node's **existing** gateway link:

- **Node conduit (PR5 of #96205):** funnel the harness's MCP into the gateway's scoped loopback tools over the node's already-authenticated WS link — **no new gateway endpoint**.
- **Hydration / "pick up anywhere" (PR7 of #96205):** materialize the **gateway-owned** conversation into a local Claude transcript so `claude --resume` continues it on whatever surface attaches.

## Why This Change Was Made

The attach grant (PR1, #96351) is transport-independent by design. Nodes are **conduits**, not new transports: a node already holds an authenticated `GatewayClient` link and can call gateway methods, so the harness's MCP rides that link instead of opening a direct connection. Hydration is the **down-mirror of PR4** (#96740): PR4 records *up* (local Claude transcript → gateway `chat.history`); PR7 hydrates *down* (gateway session → a fresh local transcript), making the gateway's `chat.history` the canonical, portable session.

## User Impact

- **Entitled node attach (admin-approved):** `node.attachGrant` mints only when the node's **owner-approved pairing permissions carry `attach`** — pairing a node for camera/canvas/system is *not* consent to reach the owner's main session + conversation, so `role:node` alone is rejected. **Granting that `attach` permission requires `operator.admin` to approve** — even for a *commandless* pairing request — so pairing/write scope can't hand a node reach into the owner's main session. Scoped to the node's **main** gateway session (bound to the session, never the node; can't widen). Consent rides the existing pairing-permissions seam (no new config), and is unforgeable: changing a node's approval surface re-triggers owner approval.
- **One command, anywhere:** `openclaw attach` auto-detects its transport — on the gateway host it mints `attach.grant` → loopback (PR2); on a paired node it runs the conduit above and `claude --resume`s the hydrated session. `--via gateway|node` forces the choice. Same command whether you're on the gateway or reaching it through a node.
- **Tools over the link:** `node.attachRelay({grantToken, mcpMessage})` → the same scoped, destructive-excluded loopback tools as the gateway host; a small loopback-TCP **forwarder** is what the harness (TCP-only) connects to.
- **Pick-up-anywhere:** `node.attachHydrate` returns the gateway-owned conversation; `prepareNodeAttach` hydrates it locally and launches `claude --resume <id>`.
- **Additive only:** new node-role methods; no change to existing gateway/node behavior, the cli-backend, or the gateway-host attach path.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests (all green):** relay core (grant → scoped tools, scope bound to the grant), node methods (grant / relay / hydrate validation), forwarder (HTTP → `node.attachRelay`, 202 notifications, 400 parse-error, 502 link-down, 405 GET, **survives a client abort mid-request**), hydration (per-turn format + **round-trips through PR4's own reader**), the `prepareNodeAttach` orchestration (grant → hydrate → forwarder → `--resume` launch); the `openclaw attach` `--via` routing + gateway→node fallback; `runNodeAttach` (connect → orchestrate → teardown); and the **admin-gated attach-permission pairing** (a commandless `attach` request denied to a pairing-only caller, approved by an `operator.admin`).
- **Hydration proven LIVE (standalone):** `claude --resume` on a hand-materialized transcript recalled a planted conversation (codeword recall, exit 0) — the format matches what Claude Code writes.
- **Full node-conduit + hydration E2E — LIVE in an isolated container** (apple-container, no Docker): a gateway, a **real node** connected over its WS link, and **real Claude Code 2.1.191**, driven by the actual `prepareNodeAttach` orchestration — **[full run log](https://gist.github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/b46a4277d88826fc22e83ab28240b3a3)**. Redacted output:
  ```text
  [e2e] NODE CONNECTED + AUTHENTICATED over the node link
  [e2e] prepareNodeAttach OK: { sessionKey: "agent:main:…", launchArgs: ["--resume","846e2ce6-…"],
                                forwarder: "http://127.0.0.1:<port>/mcp", hydratedTranscript: true }
  [e2e] launching REAL claude through the forwarder...
  [e2e] CLAUDE STDOUT >>> "The openclaw MCP server has finished connecting …" <<<
  ```
  - **Conduit:** real claude reached the gateway's `openclaw` MCP server **through the node-side forwarder, over the node's existing link** (`node.attachRelay`) — claude's own words, and `tools/list` round-tripped through it. **No new gateway endpoint.**
  - **Hydration (pick-up-anywhere):** `node.attachHydrate` returned the gateway-**owned** main-session conversation (the agent's real `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]` turns); `prepareNodeAttach` materialized it to a local transcript (44 KB, valid parentUuid chain) and claude **`--resume`d it and continued**.
  - **Entitlement gate works live:** the node minted `node.attachGrant` only because its owner-approved pairing carried `attach` — the gate rejected it (`"node is not entitled to attach"`) until the entitlement was present.
  - The scoped tool list came back **empty** — the secure default-deny for a non-owner attach on a bare gateway (no tools opted in); the conduit transport + `tools/list` round-trip are proven regardless.
- **Build, oxlint, type-check (incl. test types), deadcode, import-cycles, and topology/boundary checks all clean.** Delta on top of PR2/PR1 is the `node.attach*` methods, hydration, the `--via node` launcher path (lazy-imported so gateway-host startup stays light), and the admin-gate.

**Risks → mitigations:** the node→main-session reach is gated on an **explicit, owner-approved `attach` entitlement** (not implicit pairing), main-session-scoped (can't widen / reach other users); relay scope is sourced from the grant's `sessionKey`, never the caller; the forwarder keeps the token in the harness env (never at rest on the node) and survives a client abort without crashing the host; hydration writes a `0600` transcript. The node-conduit live E2E above is captured (real claude through the forwarder + hydrated resume).

**Follow-ups (noted, not blocking):** (1) **grant lifetime vs entitlement** — a minted grant is short-TTL'd and revocable, but outlives `attach`-permission downgrade / unpair until expiry; track per-node grant revocation on unpair/downgrade. (2) **cross-agent entitlement map** — today single-owner main-session only.

commands.author: aditya-vithaldas

target.title: fix: clarify exec tool failure warnings
target.body:
Closes #97319

AI-assisted by Codex.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users debugging failed exec/bash tool calls could see the framework-generated action summary rendered like a literal command, for example a Python script failure appearing as if the agent typed `run python3 ...`.

This affected chat/tool-failure cards and cron run diagnostics that reuse the embedded-runner payload warning text.

## Why This Change Was Made

Exec-like tool failures now render the tool label and failure state separately from the command subject, using forms like `Exec failed: <command>` instead of wrapping the whole summary before appending `failed`.

When the stored exec summary has a raw trailing inline command, the warning prefers that literal command. Known literal `run <tool> <arg>` summaries render without the framework `run` label, while semantic summaries such as package scripts, inline scripts, cwd/repo labels, node context, and pipeline summaries keep the useful display context. Non-verbose exec failures also include a concise `(exit N)` suffix when the error text exposes an exit code.

The raw metadata parser preserves explicit `node: <name>` context plus cwd labels such as `(in /tmp/build)` and `(repo)`, while avoiding duplicate/false cwd suffixes when the literal raw command itself contains parenthesized text.

## User Impact

Users get clearer failure messages when debugging failed scripts or shell commands, including which node and working directory context applied to a failed exec. The change is presentation-only; it does not change tool execution, retry policy, cron behavior, or verbose error-detail rules.

## Evidence

- Pushed follow-up commits `f89d605c`, `aed96cde`, and `9d955988` after review feedback.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/cron/run-diagnostics.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/agents/tool-display.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /tmp/codex-fast-wrapper` reported clean after the raw-detail, semantic-summary, node-context, cwd-context, compact-label, and false-positive review findings were fixed.
- Focused handler tests cover production `handleToolExecutionStart` -> `handleToolExecutionEnd` -> `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` for raw command, node context, path cwd context, and compact repo cwd context.
- Local terminal proof using production `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` with static command metadata:

```text
local raw: ⚠️ 🛠️ Exec failed: `python3 /tmp/audit.py` (exit 1)
```

commands.author: ly-wang19

target.title: fix(matrix): truncate reply context on code-point boundaries
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Matrix reply context truncation used raw UTF-16 slicing. If an emoji or other astral character straddled the reply-context limit, the quoted context could contain a dangling surrogate half before the ellipsis.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Matrix reply-context truncation now uses the existing plugin SDK `sliceUtf16Safe` helper, preserving the existing 500-code-unit cap while avoiding malformed text at the truncation boundary.

## User Impact

Matrix reply context shown to agents no longer includes replacement characters or malformed text when long messages with emoji are truncated.

## Evidence

- git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/reply-context.test.ts
- .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main

Autoreview result: clean; no accepted/actionable findings.

## After-fix Proof

Boundary probe on this PR branch:

```json
{
  "usesSafeSlice": true,
  "inputLength": 501,
  "outputLength": 499,
  "hasLoneSurrogate": false,
  "outputTail": "aaa..."
}
```

The probe places an emoji across the 497-code-unit slice boundary before the ellipsis. The after-fix output drops the emoji whole and leaves no lone UTF-16 surrogate.

target.title: [codex] Render TUI commentary progress events
target.body:
## Summary

- render `phase:"commentary"` assistant agent events in the TUI as live progress updates
- track `pendingChatRunId` before `chat.send` resolves so early Codex/OpenAI commentary can attach to the active run
- keep commentary out of final answers and chat history by leaving the existing final/history suppression path intact

## Root Cause

Codex/OpenAI progress notes arrive as assistant `agent` events with `phase:"commentary"`. The TUI only promoted normal chat deltas into visible assistant text, so commentary could be dropped before the first visible chat event. Claude appeared to work better because its visible thinking/tool streams exercised different TUI paths.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts` — passed, 3 files / 79 tests
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm check:changed` — passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` — passed
- Source-backed TUI smoke: `HOME=/Users/gregclaw pnpm openclaw tui --local --session pr-77537-smoke --message "Smoke test for PR 77537..." --timeout-ms 120000` — completed against `e52ef9d`; the TUI moved through waiting/streaming/running and displayed final text exactly `TUI_COMMENTARY_SMOKE_DONE` without commentary text in the final answer

## Rebase Notes

- Rebased onto latest `origin/main` (`a4f2bf273a` at validation time)
- Resolved the only conflict in `CHANGELOG.md` by preserving both upstream entries and this PR's TUI/progress entry
- GitHub now reports the PR as mergeable

target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->
## Hosted Marketplace Feed Stack

This is PR7 in the hosted marketplace feed stack. The stack should land in order, with maintainer review at each step before the later PRs wire hosted marketplace data into broader user flows.

1. #95846: refactor the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.
2. #95868: add the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.
3. #95877: add accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.
4. #95964: persist hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.
5. #95969: add marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
6. #95981: add configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.
7. #96155: add `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.
8. #96158: add `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.
9. #96194: add marketplace feed telemetry.

The broader follow-up after this stack is to decide whether and how marketplace entries should feed install/search/startup behavior, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->

## Summary

Adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh`, a manual activation slice for the hosted marketplace feed stack. The command reads `config.marketplaces`, calls the configured hosted external plugin catalog loader, writes the accepted snapshot, and reports whether the accepted catalog came from the hosted feed, the last-known-good snapshot, or bundled fallback.

This gives maintainers a safe way to exercise the live `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins` path and any locally configured `marketplaces.feeds` profile without changing startup, onboarding, doctor, install, search, or provider-repair behavior yet.

## What Changed

- Adds `plugins marketplace refresh` with:
  - `--feed-profile <name>` for a configured marketplace feed profile.
  - `--feed-url <url>` for the existing guarded explicit hosted URL path.
  - `--expected-sha256 <hash>` for checksum-pinned refresh checks.
  - `--json` for automation-friendly source/feed/metadata output.
- Keeps the command lazy-loaded with the existing plugin runtime boundary.
- Reports hosted, hosted-snapshot, and bundled-fallback results.
- Fails checksum-pinned refreshes when the accepted result is not a fresh hosted payload, so automation cannot silently accept bundled fallback after a checksum mismatch.
- Normalizes bare and prefixed SHA-256 pins to the loader checksum format.
- Rejects explicit hosted feed URLs with credentials, query strings, or fragments before fetch/snapshot persistence.
- Keeps direct default ClawHub URL refresh behavior aligned with the default profile, while requiring manifest `sourceRef` for non-default direct feed URLs.
- Updates the plugin CLI reference docs for `plugins marketplace refresh`.
- Adds focused tests for CLI routing, refresh output, fallback reporting, redacted feed URL output, checksum normalization, URL rejection, source-ref enforcement, and pinned-refresh failure.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts docs/cli/plugins.md`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `$env:CI='1'; node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (8 CLI refresh/lazy tests and 51 catalog tests passed)
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (no actionable correctness issues)

Note: `pnpm format:docs:check` still fails on this Windows host before checking this file because the repo-wide docs formatter builds an `oxfmt` command line that exceeds the Windows command-line length limit. The targeted `oxfmt --check docs/cli/plugins.md` and `pnpm docs:check-mdx` passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` is the first manual activation path for hosted marketplace feeds. It accepts a fresh hosted payload and writes the accepted snapshot without enabling install/search/startup consumption.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\src\openclaw-prflow\.worktrees\pr-96155` at pushed PR7 head `1614070e92` (runtime proof path unchanged from `034ab82b95`; current head adds docs-only refresh-behavior clarification), source CLI runtime harness via `node --import tsx`, isolated state dir `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr96155-refresh-034ab82`, live HTTPS request to `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Set runtime config snapshot with `marketplaces.feeds.clawhub-public.url = https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.
2. Set source profiles `public-clawhub.type = clawhub` and `public-npm.type = npm`.
3. Ran `runPluginMarketplaceRefreshCommand({ feedProfile: "clawhub-public", json: true })`.
4. Reused the same `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and called `loadConfiguredHostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEntries(config, { feedProfile: "clawhub-public", offline: true })` to verify the refresh wrote a durable snapshot.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "source": "hosted",
  "entries": 59,
  "metadata": {
    "url": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins",
    "status": 200,
    "etag": "\"sha256:76a49aad745fe828c7f88b5a870ad67b5953c5235b4c928b1d6189584b257b63-gzip\"",
    "lastModified": "Sun, 28 Jun 2026 12:47:16 GMT",
    "checksum": "sha256:76a49aad745fe828c7f88b5a870ad67b5953c5235b4c928b1d6189584b257b63"
  },
  "feed": {
    "id": "clawhub-official",
    "generatedAt": "2026-06-28T12:47:09.109Z",
    "sequence": 19
  }
}
```

Offline snapshot verification from the same state dir:
```json
{
  "proofHead": "034ab82b95",
  "stateDir": "C:\\tmp\\openclaw-feeds-proof\\pr96155-refresh-034ab82",
  "offlineSource": "hosted-snapshot",
  "offlineEntries": 59,
  "offlineFeed": {
    "id": "clawhub-official",
    "sequence": 19
  },
  "offlineChecksum": "sha256:76a49aad745fe828c7f88b5a870ad67b5953c5235b4c928b1d6189584b257b63"
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The refresh command accepted the live hosted ClawHub payload, reported `source: hosted`, returned 59 entries, and persisted a snapshot that the loader could read back offline from the same state directory.

What was not tested:
This proof does not install plugins from the refreshed feed. Installation, search, startup consumption, feed auth, and signed-feed enforcement are later slices.

target.title: Doctor: expose systemd linger findings
target.body:
## Summary

- add default-disabled structured lint findings for `core/doctor/systemd-linger`
- keep legacy interactive/non-interactive linger enablement in the existing Doctor run paths
- gate structured linger findings on the same loaded Gateway systemd service precondition as the legacy Doctor path, so `--all`/`--only` stays quiet on hosts without an OpenClaw systemd user service

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (52 tests)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

`node scripts/check-changed.mjs` did not complete locally because the wrapper delegates to `pnpm crabbox:run`, and pnpm stopped before execution with `[ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY]` while trying to purge/reinstall `node_modules` in this non-TTY WSL worktree.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Selected `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/systemd-linger` should not warn merely because local Linux linger is disabled. It should only report disabled linger when OpenClaw is actually running through a loaded Gateway systemd user service, matching the legacy Doctor path.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 root checkout at PR head `2fe7e3add2e275e287ee69d877e7939c144c8e9f`, source CLI `./openclaw.mjs`, local Linux mode. The host has `loginctl` reporting `Linger=no`, but the systemd user service/session is not active for the Gateway path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `./openclaw.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/systemd-linger > /tmp/systemd-linger-proof.json; code=$?; printf "EXIT=%s\n" "$code"; cat /tmp/systemd-linger-proof.json`
2. `systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway`
3. `loginctl show-user root -p Linger`
4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix:
```text
EXIT=0
{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":32,"findings":[]}

systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway
Failed to connect to bus: No such file or directory

loginctl show-user root -p Linger
Linger=no

Vitest: src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts passed, 52 tests.
```

Observed result after fix:
The selected structured lint check ran and stayed quiet on a Linux host with `Linger=no` when no Gateway systemd user service was loaded. Focused source tests also cover the positive loaded-service disabled-linger warning path and the not-loaded quiet path; the quiet path asserts `readSystemdUserLingerStatus` is not called when the Gateway service is not loaded.

What was not tested:
A real non-WSL Linux host with an active loaded OpenClaw Gateway systemd user service was not available here, so disabled-linger and enabled-linger service-loaded CLI transcripts remain source-harness covered rather than real-host covered.

## Contract

No config schema, plugin manifest, SDK, persisted data model, or repair-path contract change. This adds only an opt-in structured Doctor lint finding for existing systemd-linger health behavior.

target.title: feat(media): force Telegram image generation as documents
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram users receiving `image_generate` results could get generated images through photo-style delivery, which can recompress media and make generated image artifacts less reliable to save, inspect, or forward as original files.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change treats Telegram `image_generate` outputs as document/file media where supported. It keeps the existing direct Telegram delivery behavior and also propagates `forceDocument` through source-reply, block-reply, queued tool-media, and outbound delivery payload paths so the normal active generated-media reply path preserves the document-delivery preference too.

The change is intentionally scoped to delivery metadata. It does not alter image generation providers, media file creation, or non-Telegram channel behavior.

AI-assisted: yes; the final patch was reviewed with focused local tests and type/lint checks.

## User Impact

Telegram users should receive generated image outputs as document/file attachments instead of recompressed photo messages. That makes image-generation artifacts more faithful to the generated file and avoids losing file/document affordances in Telegram.

## Evidence

Local verification on commit `ffb71a9a42b3c9933215dc2141efd0157f03b2a9`:

- `git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <18 changed files>` passed.
- Focused tests passed: 357 tests across:
  - `src/agents/runtime-plan/types.compat.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` passed.
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts` passed.
- `pnpm check:test-types` passed.
- Targeted oxlint on the changed TypeScript files passed with 0 warnings / 0 errors.

Operational note: full local `pnpm lint` was started but the small VM killed the extensions shard with SIGKILL after the core shard passed. `pnpm check:changed` attempted Blacksmith Testbox delegation, but this local environment does not have the `blacksmith` executable installed. CI should run those hosted gates on this updated head.

Native Telegram behavior proof is still required by repository policy; requesting a Mantis Telegram Desktop proof run in PR comments.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `448d4813e8f`: fix the plugin-name compatibility on the REAL plugin path (addresses ClawSweeper [P1]).** The previous fix preserved a same-named plugin only at the final gateway deny filter, but the production plugin loader filters `pluginToolDenylist` names first — so a same-named plugin `read`/`write`/`edit` was still dropped before that filter (the bot was right; the prior unit proof passed only because the mocked `createOpenClawTools` ignored the denylist). Fix: in `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`, the host-FS coding names are now excluded from the `pluginToolDenylist` handed to the loader **when they are denied only by the host-FS default** (explicit `gateway.tools.deny` / owner-only still deny the plugin); the built-in stays gated at the final filter. The test mock now **mirrors the production loader** (drops a plugin-owned tool whose name is in `pluginToolDenylist`), so the regression tests exercise the real path. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container — **red/green**: reverting the exclusion makes the two plugin-compat tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`; with the fix they pass. The real resolver output was confirmed directly (the `pluginToolDenylist` it passes excludes `read`/`write`/`edit` in the host-FS-default case, includes a name under explicit `gateway.tools.deny`). Full gate green: `tools-invoke-http` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http` 19/19, `schema.help.quality` 23/23, `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` clean, **`tsgo:core` typecheck clean** (also fixed pre-existing `defaultGatewayDeny`/`ownerOnlyGatewayDeny` `.includes(string)` type errors my earlier `tsc` check had missed), oxlint clean.

---

> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `228ade8544e`: rebased the full stack onto current `main`.** Conflict resolved (`scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` surface budgets reconciled to main's current values + this stack's deltas; `config-types` deprecated budget 416→417 for the new public `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type). The plugin-compat [P1] fix (source-aware host-FS default-deny) survives the rebase and is **re-proven against current main**: in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container, reverting only the resolver fix makes the two plugin-compat tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`, and restoring it passes — so the regression exists on current main and the fix removes it. Full gate green on the rebased head: `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` 19/19, `schema.help.quality.test.ts` 23/23, `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` clean, oxlint clean, typecheck clean. Now `MERGEABLE` against main. The host-FS write boundary still needs explicit maintainer/security acceptance (a product decision, not an author fix).

---

> **Update 2026-06-28 — head `acb9ab0a39d`: fix ClawSweeper [P1] plugin-name compatibility.** Adding `read`/`write`/`edit` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` name-denied any *same-named plugin tool* over `/tools/invoke` + SDK `tools.invoke`, so a pre-existing non-optional plugin `read`/`write`/`edit` returned 404 even with the host-FS opt-ins off. Per the resolver, the default-deny is now **source-aware** (`src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` `HOST_FS_BUILTIN_CODING_DENY_NAMES` + the gateway deny filter in `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): a same-named tool whose *only* deny reason is the host-FS built-in coding default is preserved when it carries plugin metadata (`getPluginToolMeta`); the built-in coding tool (no plugin meta) stays gated behind the owner gate + opt-in, and owner-only / explicit `gateway.tools.deny` / `exec`/`fs_write`/etc. are unaffected. Two regression tests assert a plugin `read`/`write` stays reachable **without** a new `gateway.tools.allow` entry. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container: `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` 176/176, `audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` 19/19, `schema.help.quality.test.ts` 23/23; oxlint clean. Red/green proof: reverting only the resolver fix makes both new tests fail with `expected 404 to be 200`. **Still pending: rebase onto current `main`** (main refactored the gateway deny-handling path, so the rebase needs careful re-expression — tracked separately).

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `4fe6f873c9` (scope owner gate to built-in host-FS write tools; restacked on #85664 `d625c6de23`).** Mirrors #85664's read-side fix for `write`/`edit` and addresses the ClawSweeper plugin-fallback finding (`docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` write section). Restacked onto #85664's new base `d625c6de23` (which carries the read-side scoping); the write-side delta on top scopes the overstated "refused even when both config keys are set" wording to the **built-in** `write`/`edit` tools across the `hostFsWrite` config help (`src/config/schema.help.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` member comment (`src/config/types.gateway.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` bullet in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and the write section of `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` (prose, truth table, _Plugin name collision_ note). Per the resolver (`src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): for a non-owner caller the built-in is never materialized, but a same-named allowlisted plugin tool still resolves under the normal `gateway.tools.allow`/`deny` policy. Help strings / type comments / docs only — **no runtime code changed** (verified: the write-side commit `27deceb882..4fe6f873c9` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, and the two `docs/gateway/*.md` files — no `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, or `schema.labels.ts`), so the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `pnpm check:docs` (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links) and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `61e6f29bed` (config-help owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]/[P3]).** Restacked onto #85664's new base `7f9876c2b2`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `661d6838c0` is **config help / type docs** (verified: `git diff 661d6838c0 61e6f29bed` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts` +4 and `src/config/types.gateway.ts` +25 — comment/string lines; every runtime file under `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, `schema.labels.ts` is byte-identical): the `hostFsWrite` `FIELD_HELP` string and the `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary + `hostFsWrite` member comment now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces, and the type summary is refreshed so it no longer calls `hostFsWrite` "future work". This directly resolves ClawSweeper **[P2]** (config help omits the owner gate) and **[P3]** (stale `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary). Because no runtime code changed, the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) and `pnpm check:docs` both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `661d6838c0` (docs owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]).** Restacked onto #85664's docs-updated read base `ca46eb9946`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `afe36ccf45` is **docs**: the `read` + `write`/`edit` truth tables in `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` and the `hostFsRead`/`hostFsWrite` bullets in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces — directly resolving ClawSweeper's **[P2]** ("Document the owner gate for file-tool opt-ins"). No source files changed (verified: `git diff afe36ccf45 661d6838c0` touches only those two docs files), so `dist/` is byte-identical and the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. `pnpm check:docs` passes in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links).

---

Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the `resolveGatewayScopedTools` resolver). Same **triple-key owner-gated** shape as #85664's `read`: a surface-specific class opt-in **plus** the per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` **plus** `senderIsOwner === true`, all on the `http` surface.

## Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base (head `afe36ccf456f`, base upstream `023ce6e96c38`)

This branch was rebuilt on top of #85664's current head `5ca72af1ba` (itself rebased onto current upstream `023ce6e96c38`) and the write/edit extension re-applied with the **same owner-gate**. This directly resolves the prior ClawSweeper [P1].

**Bot [P1] (resolved):** upstream commit `02182d5a3031` ("refactor: remove sender owner tool gating") deleted the `ownerOnly` enforcement helper, so the earlier `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag this PR added became dead code — a trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller could have reached an allowlisted mutating tool with no owner check. **Fix:** this PR no longer relies on that tag. `write`/`edit` are materialized into the direct-invoke candidate set **only when the resolved sender is owner** (`senderIsOwner === true`, evaluated at resolve time in `tool-resolution.ts`), exactly mirroring #85664's read gate. A trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller is now refused even with both config keys set (proven below, cases 2 & 4).

## Triple-key gating

To reach a write-class tool on the direct-invoke surface, ALL must hold:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. | `false` |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` includes the tool name | Per-tool enablement. | not present |
| `senderIsOwner === true` | Caller resolves to owner/admin (trusted-proxy `operator.admin`, or shared-secret bearer). | — |

Truth table (tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`, surface = `http`):

| `allow` ∋ `T` | `hostFsWrite` | sender owner | `T` reachable |
|---|---|---|---|
| no  | no  | —   | ❌ |
| yes | no  | yes | ❌ (class opt-in off; built-in not materialized; `dangerous_allow` audit fires) |
| no  | yes | yes | ❌ (not in allow) |
| yes | yes | **no**  | ❌ (**owner gate** — refused) |
| yes | yes | yes | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (behind `hostFsWrite: true` + allow + owner).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` + allow + owner.
- **`apply_patch`:** present in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for defense-in-depth, but the coding-tools factory produces no `apply_patch` entry, so it is never materialized (proven below, case 7).
- **NOT exposed:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` — RCE-class; out of scope for this PR.

## Audit

- `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` fires only when BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` AND a write-class name is in `allow` (mirrors `host_read_allow`); escalates to `critical` on non-loopback bind.
- `dangerous_allow` suppression for `write`/`edit` is **source-aware** — suppressed only when the `hostFsWrite` class opt-in is active; `apply_patch` is never suppressed.

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — `hostFsWrite` schema entry
- `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — field help + label
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — owner-gated write/edit materialization; coding built-ins win plugin name collision when class opt-in active
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `host_write_allow` finding; source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression
- `src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — write coding-tool collision precedence (owner→built-in, non-owner→plugin, opt-in-off→plugin)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — `host_write_allow` dual-key + source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression cases (incl. `apply_patch` never suppressed)
- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — coding-tool factory plumbing
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`, `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — `hostFsWrite` opt-in, triple-key truth tables, `host_write_allow` catalog row

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Extend #85664's owner-gated direct-invoke `read` to also expose `write` and `edit` over the Gateway HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke` surfaces, behind the same triple key (class opt-in `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` + per-tool `gateway.tools.allow` + `senderIsOwner`). Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base so write/edit materialize only for an owner sender — resolving the prior [P1] where upstream `02182d5a3031` made the old `ownerOnly` tag dead code.

**Real environment tested:** Built fresh from head `afe36ccf456f` inside a `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (full runtime build → `dist/index.js`; SDK → `packages/sdk/dist/index.mjs`). Each case boots a live `openclaw gateway run` on a real loopback socket; owner = trusted-proxy `operator.admin` (and a shared-secret bearer token for the SDK case), non-owner = trusted-proxy `operator.write`. Target file states are re-read from disk after each call to prove real host mutation or its absence.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** `PROOF_HEAD=afe36ccf456f bash .proof-63919.sh` inside the container — boots nine gateway configs and dispatches `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` over HTTP `/tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke`, asserting the HTTP status and re-reading each target file from disk.

**Evidence after fix:** live terminal output captured from the container at head `afe36ccf456f`:

```
=== HEAD: afe36ccf456f ===

### LIVE PROOF (HTTP trusted-proxy surface) ###

--- 1 W-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 24 bytes to /work/.proof63/w1.txt"}]}}
  file=OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f
--- 2 W-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=PRESEED
--- 3 E-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /work/.proof63/e3.txt."}],"details":{"diff":" 1 ALPHA\\n-2 BETA\\n+2 GAMMA-afe36ccf456f", ...}}}
  file=ALPHA / GAMMA-afe36ccf456f
--- 4 E-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
  file=ALPHA / BETA  (unchanged)
--- 5 W-DENIED-no-opt-in (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=false, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 6 W-DENIED-no-allow (allow=[] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 7 APPLYPATCH-denied (allow=[apply_patch] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=apply_patch) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: apply_patch"}}
--- 8 READ-REGRESSION (allow=[read] hostFsRead=true, operator.admin, tool=read) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"READ-BASE-OK-afe36ccf456f\\n"}]}}

### LIVE PROOF (SDK token surface — shared-secret bearer = owner) ###

--- 9 SDK-W-OWNER (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, token bearer, tool=write) ---
  [SDK token] {"ok":true,"toolName":"write","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 28 bytes to /work/.proof63/w9.txt"}]},"source":"core"}
  file=SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=afe36ccf456f ===
```

Independent re-read of persisted file states after the run: `w1.txt`=`OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`, `w2.txt`=`PRESEED` (non-owner write left it untouched), `e3.txt`=`ALPHA/GAMMA-afe36ccf456f`, `e4.txt`=`ALPHA/BETA` (non-owner edit left it untouched), `w5.txt` and `w6.txt` absent (never created), `w9.txt`=`SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`.

**Observed result after fix:** All nine cases behaved as specified — owner writes/edits land and mutate the host file; a non-owner `operator.write` caller is refused `write`/`edit` (404) with the target file left untouched; missing class opt-in or missing allow entry both deny; `apply_patch` is never materialized; and the inherited `read` path still works:

| # | Case | Config | Caller | HTTP/SDK | File state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | write owner | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 wrote 24 bytes | written |
| 2 | write **non-owner** | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 3 | edit owner | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 replaced 1 block | edited |
| 4 | edit **non-owner** | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 5 | write no opt-in | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=false | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 6 | write no allow | allow=[], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 7 | apply_patch | allow=[apply_patch], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | factory never materializes it |
| 8 | read regression | allow=[read], hostFsRead=true | `operator.admin` | 200 + contents | #85664 base intact |
| 9 | write owner (SDK) | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | token bearer | ok=true wrote 28 bytes | written |

Supplemental (not a substitute for the live proof above), all in the same `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container at head `afe36ccf456f`: unit tests — **160 passed** across the gateway test projects (`tools-invoke-http` 42 + `tool-resolution` 5 + `audit-gateway-tools-http` 19, ×3 projects = 7 file-projects); `lint` (oxlint, 0/0), `tsgo:core` (0), `check:test-types` (0), and `plugin-sdk-surface-report` vitest (4/4). **`checks-node-core-fast` / plugin-SDK surface budget:** this PR adds `hostFsWrite?` as a *field* on the existing `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type — **zero new top-level exports** — so `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` passes on the `+1` budget already carried by #85664's base commit (`config-types` 416 / public 10285 / public-fn 5164 / deprecated 3238). No further budget change in this PR.

**Not tested:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` (RCE-class; out of scope for this PR and never exposed by the direct-invoke factory).

target.title: docs: clarify `/reset` is not an alias for `/new`
target.body:
Fixes #76592.

This updates the slash command docs and native command summaries so `/new` and `/reset` are no longer presented as interchangeable aliases.

- `/new [model]` starts a new session.
- `/reset` resets the current session in place.

The old wording called `/reset` the reset alias, which made it sound interchangeable with `/new`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The PR removes user-facing alias-like wording for `/new` and `/reset`. After this patch, the docs say `/new` starts a new session and `/reset` resets the current session in place; ACP/native command summaries now say the same thing.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 26.3.1 OpenClaw runtime host with OpenClaw `2026.5.3-1 (2eae30e)` available, plus this PR branch checked out locally at `893e25f76e285c74b37a79cac2b579aa6936bc90`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran terminal commands against the patched branch to read the exact user-facing strings that changed:

```text
sed -n '125,128p' docs/tools/slash-commands.md
sed -n '23,24p' src/acp/commands.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the patched branch:

```text
$ sed -n '125,128p' docs/tools/slash-commands.md
    - `/new [model]` starts a new session. `/reset` resets the current session in place; it is not an alias for `/new`.
    - Control UI intercepts typed `/new` to create and switch to a fresh dashboard session; typed `/reset` still runs the Gateway's in-place reset.

$ sed -n '23,24p' src/acp/commands.ts
  { name: "reset", description: "Reset the current session in place." },
  { name: "new", description: "Start a new session." },
```

**Observed result after fix:** The after-fix output shows both affected user-facing surfaces now distinguish `/new` from `/reset` and no longer describe either command as the other's alias.

**What was not tested:** No additional gaps.

This is a docs/user-facing command-copy fix only. It does not change command dispatch behavior.

## Testing

- Ran `git diff --check`.
- Verified the updated docs line and ACP command summaries with the terminal output shown above.
- Did not run `pnpm build`, `pnpm check`, or `pnpm test` because this changes only documentation and command descriptions.

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex to locate the contradictory wording, prepare the docs patch, and align the related command summaries after review feedback.
